name: Shipping / Tanker Rates
summary: Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE PHYSICAL FLOOR HELD THROUGH THE CASUALTY-DAY ESCALATION — STRAIT STILL ~5% OF PRE-WAR TRAFFIC, WAR-RISK INSURANCE NOT UNWINDING; PAPER (BOAT -0.4%, FRONTLINE +0.2%) FLAT.** No confirmed new attack on neutral commercial shipping despite the 3 June barrage and the renewed Bab-el-Mandeb 'activation' rhetoric (a MARAD advisory 2026-006 / UKMTO note elevated risk; one gCaptain report of a merchant vessel repelling an armed approach off Yemen, but no confirmed Houthi strike). Hormuz remains effectively shut (~5% of pre-war ~3,000 vessels/month, ~150 tankers anchored outside). VLCC TD3C ~$474K/day on the last Baltic print (~4x pre-war); war-risk insurance (Hormuz) ~$10-14M/transit, 2.5-5% of hull (~40-60x pre-war), NOT unwinding — JWC now treats the whole Persian Gulf as high-risk. Equity proxy flat (BOAT $40.67 -0.4%; Frontline $34.74 +0.2%). The slow physical legs are the structural floor under oil's premium; they lag 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal. No trigger moves. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE PHYSICAL FLOOR HELD THROUGH THE RE-ESCALATION — STRAIT STILL ~4 SHIPS/DAY, WAR-RISK INSURANCE NOT UNWINDING, VLCC AG WAR PREMIUM INTACT; PAPER WHIPSAWED ±7% ON THE OIL. The strait is effectively closed at ~4 vessels/day vs ~95 normal (CNN '94 days of paralysis'); blockade enforcement continued — US fired a Hellfire into a commercial ship's engine room (continued enforcement; may be the same Lian-Star-class interdiction or a fresh vessel — do NOT double-count the blockade tally without confirmation). VLCC AG-China ~$92-100K/day, stable, ~3x WAFR/USG (Baltic/Breakwave 2 June) — Arabian Gulf war premium extraordinary, Atlantic basin softening. War-risk insurance (Hormuz) still $10-14M/transit, 2.5-5%, ~60x pre-crisis, NOT unwinding (Lloyd's List/Howden; DFC $40bn reinsurance backstop). Container freight (FBX) elevated on Red Sea reroutes (Asia-N.Eur ~$2,707/FEU; Med ~$3,850). Equity proxy: BOAT $40.69 (-0.2% 1D / -1.7% MoM), Frontline $34.62 (-0.7% 1D / -6.5% MoM) — paper-physical split intact (paper whipsawed on the Brent +7%-then-fade; physical book locked). No confirmed new Houthi/Axis attack on neutral shipping this window — only rhetoric (Bab el-Mandeb 'activation'). The slow physical legs (insurance, freight) are the structural floor under oil's ~$30 premium; they lag 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal. No trigger moves. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30). **THE BLOCKADE IS STILL INTERDICTING IRAN-BOUND TRAFFIC — 6th VESSEL STOPPED — AND HORMUZ REMAINS MINED, BUT THE PAPER FREIGHT TAPE IS FADING THE DEAL. The US disabled the Gambia-flagged Lian Star in the Gulf of Oman (6th vessel stopped during the blockade); a new suspected floating mine was sighted off Oman (30 May). The slow physical legs have NOT unwound: VLCC volumes -36% (longer voyages), war-risk insurance still 3-8% of hull (~8x pre-war) — but no fresh late-May VLCC/AWRP print this window (freshest hard VLCC data ~Week 16, late April; carried, not reconfirmed). Equity proxy faded: BOAT -0.5% 1D / -1.4% MoM, Frontline +0.4% 1D / -4.4% MoM (paper deal-fade vs physical book still locked). The paper-vs-physical bifurcation persists; the fast legs (oil, fertilizer, LNG) de-priced first, the slow legs (insurance, freight) lag 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28). **PHYSICAL VOLUMES IMPAIRED, PAPER SOFTENING ON DEAL HOPES — VLCC GULF-CHINA ~$100K/DAY ($91.7K INDEX), STABILIZED ~3 WEEKS; CRUDE-ON-VLCC VOLUMES STILL -36% VS PREWAR; THE STRAIT HAS BEEN SHUT 71+ DAYS (DAY 90 OF THE BLOCKADE). FRONTLINE $34.71 (-2.1% 1D, -3.9% MoM, -9.6% 3M) — TANKER SPOT EQUITIES SOFTENING ON DEAL-ANTICIPATION (Lloyd's List / Baltic Exchange).** The bifurcation holds: paper tanker equities keep bleeding on deal hopes (the 60-day MOU awaits Trump's signature; Trump 'no sanctions relief'; nuclear 'deadlock'; WH called Iran's state-TV draft a 'complete fabrication') while the PHYSICAL leg is structurally impaired — -36% crude-on-VLCC volumes intact and rates holding ~$100K/day (double last-year's spot, NOT escalating toward the $200K+ divergence terminal). Today's tape was de-escalatory but not peace: the IRGC fired one calibrated ballistic missile at a US airbase in Kuwait (intercepted, zero casualty) and the US executed a SECOND defensive strike (4 Iranian drones near Hormuz + a ground-control station near Bandar Abbas) — physical risk active inside the ceasefire, so insurers/shippers will not rush back. **Investor read: the tanker longs that worked Feb-Apr have given back materially on paper (FRO -9.6% 3M); the physical Q2 book ($181,700/day VLCC TCE, 82% covered) prints regardless — if the deal signs cleanly, physical takes a quarter to unwind; if it breaks, FRO/BOAT gap back up first.** The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude fired again (paper softens on deal hopes / volumes -36% not unwinding). PRIOR (May 27): **THE PAPER TANKER TRADE UNWOUND (FRONTLINE -3.0%, BOAT -2.0%) WHILE THE PHYSICAL LEG STAYED INTACT — VLCC VOLUMES STILL -36% THROUGH HORMUZ (14.4m bpd vs 22.5m prewar), RATES HIGH, FRONTLINE Q2 TCE LOCKED AT $181,700/DAY (82% COVERAGE). THE EASY CRISIS-BETA TRADE IS OVER; WHAT'S LEFT IS THE BINARY ON DEAL SIGNATURE (C6).** Deal-anticipation + Iran non-retaliation (the US strikes May 25 went unanswered) → the EQUITY expression of the crisis trade bled (FRO -3.0% / BOAT -2.0%), but the PHYSICAL stack priced ZERO operational change: -36% volumes intact, war-risk insurance still ~0.8-1% of hull (peaks 5%+) vs 0.2% pre-war NOT unwinding, Q2 contracted book bid above spot. C6 mechanism: even on a clean signed deal, insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage' (the May 25 strikes on IRGC mine-laying boats show physical risk is active inside the ceasefire) — the physical cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, so the equity-relief bounce front-runs real relief by weeks. **The investor read: the tanker longs that worked Feb-Apr have given back materially on paper; the physical Q2 book prints regardless — if the deal signs, physical takes a quarter to unwind; if it breaks (Iranian rejection/retaliation OR a Beirut strike), FRO/BOAT gap back up first.** The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude fired again (paper unwound / volumes -36% not unwinding). PRIOR (May 25): **VLCC VOLUMES STILL -36% THROUGH HORMUZ (LLOYD'S LIST), RATES STABILISED ~$100K/DAY — THE PHYSICAL SHIPPING LEG DID NOT UNWIND ON THE DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE (C4 NON-CONFIRMATION). Today paper priced the US-Iran deal as near-done (Brent <$100, TTF -4.2%, urea -7.4%, India +1.3%), but the physical tanker market priced ZERO operational change: -36% volumes intact (14.4m bpd avg vs 22.5m prewar), war-risk insurance still 3-8% of hull (8x+ NOT unwinding), Frontline Q2 contracted book 82% @ $181,700/day VLCC TCE. Rate-stabilisation at $100K/day (NOT continued escalation toward the $200K+ 'paper-physical divergence terminal' threshold) is consistent with the physical-tanker market also pricing the deal at high probability on the marginal-rate axis — but NOT on the VOLUME axis, which remains structurally cut -36%. C4 mechanism: even on a clean signed deal, insurers and shippers will not rush back into a strait Iran still claims to 'manage'; the physical cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, so the equity-relief bounce front-runs real relief by weeks. Frontline ($37.12) / BOAT ($42.00) FROZEN at Friday closes (US Memorial Day — NYSE did not settle; Friday's FRO -3.4% / BOAT -2.6% were paper deal-fade, not a Monday move). The non-confirmation edge to brent-crude and marine-war-risk-insurance fired today as a divergence signal. Reversibility: paper component reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli kinetic action; physical Q2 book is contracted regardless.** PRIOR (May 24): **VLCC RATES STABILISE AT ~$100K/DAY (NOT ESCALATING TO $200K+ DIVERGENCE TERMINAL); HORMUZ VLCC VOLUMES STILL -36% VS PREWAR (LLOYD'S LIST MAY 22 PRIMARY). PAPER-PHYSICAL DIVERGENCE PERSISTS — PAPER SOLD THE DEAL ANTICIPATION (FRO -3.4% FRIDAY, BOAT -2.6% FRIDAY) BUT PHYSICAL CONTRACTED BOOK INTACT (FRONTLINE Q2 BOOKED 82% @ $181,700/DAY VLCC TCE). RATE-STABILISATION AT $100K/DAY (vs CONTINUED ESCALATION) IS CONSISTENT WITH DEAL-ANTICIPATION TRADE IN PHYSICAL SHIPPING — TANKER OPERATORS PAID FOR BALLAST/DIVERSION BUT NOT PRICING ESCALATION PREMIUM BEYOND CURRENT VOYAGE EXPOSURES.** Per Lloyd's List May 22 reporting: VLCC volumes dropped 36% versus prewar levels — global crude exports on VLCCs averaged 14.4m bpd over past eight weeks, down 8.1m bpd from prewar. VLCC rates 'stabilised around $100,000 per day, double spot rates at this time last year' — Baltic Exchange US Gulf-China index $91,731/day and West Africa-China $99,407/day. **The strait remains effectively closed for past 71+ days.** Frontline's Q1 record TCE of $103,500/day (Friday close $37.12) and Q2 booked 82% @ $181,700/day remain unchanged on Sunday. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (8x pre-war) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape; physical premium intact. **The rate stabilisation at $100K/day — rather than continued escalation toward $200K+ 'paper-physical divergence terminal' threshold — is the cleanest signal that physical-tanker market is also pricing the deal at high probability**, but only on the marginal-rate axis (NOT on the volume axis, which remains -36%). **Reversibility**: paper component reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli unilateral kinetic action; physical Q2 book is contracted regardless. PRIOR (May 23): FRONTLINE $37.12 MAY 22 CLOSE (-3.4% 1D, +6.4% 1M, +9.1% 3M) ON Q1 2026 EARNINGS RELEASE: RECORD VLCC TCE $103,500/DAY + TCE EARNINGS $424.5M +71% QoQ; BUT Q2 GUIDANCE FLAGGED BALLAST-DAY WEAKNESS AGAINST CONTRACTED $181,700/DAY VLCC BOOK ON 82% COVERAGE. PAPER MARKET PRICED THE Q2 FORWARD-RATE SOFTENING; PHYSICAL BOOK REMAINS STRUCTURALLY BID. PAPER vs PHYSICAL DIVERGENCE AT CYCLE EXTREME. BOAT $42.00 -2.6% 1D — PAPER-ETF SYMPATHY.** **Causal chain (markets dossier C4)**: Q1 record VLCC TCE $103,500/day, Suezmax $72,400, LR2/Aframax $50,700 — TCE earnings $424.5M (+71% QoQ); BUT Q2 forward booking 82% of VLCC days contracted at $181,700/day (this is +75% above Q1 — physical book structurally bid). Q2 GUIDANCE flag: 'spot TCEs for the full Q2 will be LOWER than the spot TCEs currently contracted, due to the impact of ballast days during Q2' → paper market read 'Q2 paper rate weaker than $181,700 ceiling' → sold the stock -3.4%. **Baltic Exchange Week 21** (May 22): VLCC TD3C (Mideast Gulf → China) WS402.5, -45 pts vs prior Friday; TCE round-trip ~$397,753/day. TD22 (US Gulf → China) TCE ~$106,000/day, fell ~$160K voyage WoW — Atlantic basin softening. **Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer'** — 36% volume cut at extended distances re-prices freight market structurally. Atlantic-to-Pacific share of VLCC volumes now 35% (vs 22% Jan-Feb 2026 baseline). **The physical book is bid; paper is being sold.** Cash gen at current TC rates and TCE = $1.5B / ~$7 per share. **Frontline's price move is misleading as crisis-de-escalation signal**: stock fell because forward-curve paper is softening, NOT because structural Hormuz disruption is easing. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (8x pre-war) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape. **Reversibility**: Paper component reverses on kinetic event reigniting forward curve. Physical book is contracted regardless. **IRGC 25-vessel Hormuz claim May 23** (CLAIMED, contradicted by Bloomberg/Lloyd's/UKMTO) does NOT change physical-shipping landscape — propaganda lock-in for Iran-Oman toll mechanism. PRIOR (May 22): **FRONTLINE Q1 RESULTS MAY 22 — AVG DAILY SPOT TCE Q1 $103,500/DAY FOR VLCCs; Q2 CONTRACTED TCEs $181,700/DAY WITH 82% COVERAGE = +75% SEQUENTIAL. CEO LARS BARSTAD ON CALL: 'HORMUZ CHAOS SUPERCHARGED TANKER PROFITS.' ATLANTIC-TO-PACIFIC SHARE OF VLCC VOLUMES NOW 35% (VS 22% JAN-FEB 2026 BASELINE) — TON-MILES DISLOCATION OPERATIONALLY VALIDATED AT FUNDAMENTAL SCALE. LLOYD'S LIST: 'HORMUZ CRISIS SLASHES VLCC VOLUMES BY 36% BUT VOYAGES ARE LONGER.'** **Frontline -3.0% 1D / BOAT -2.3% 1D = PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION REVERSED DIRECTION, INTENSIFIED. Paper sold the deal anticipation; physical confirmed the cycle peak.** **Paper tape**: Frontline -3.0% 1D, BOAT -2.3% 1D = deal-anticipation paper unwind; same logic as energy: if Hormuz reopens, ton-miles compress, day rates fall. **Physical fundamentals (DIAMETRICALLY OPPOSITE)**: Frontline Q1 spot TCE $103.5K/day → Q2 contracted TCE $181.7K/day (+75% sequential) for VLCCs with 82% coverage (Manila Times / GlobeNewswire May 22 primary); 36% volume cut at extended voyage distances re-prices freight market STRUCTURALLY (Lloyd's List May 22 'Hormuz crisis slashes VLCC volumes by 36% but voyages are longer'). **The cheniere-energy edge (XLE-Frontline-Cheniere thesis) is operationally validated on the Frontline axis** — Cheniere Q1 dividend $0.555/share paid May 19 + $1.2B deployed Q1 (consistent with sustained capital deployment off elevated EBITDA guide). **Paper-vs-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 has now resolved IN FAVOR OF PHYSICAL** — the Q2 guide is for ACTUAL contracted business, not a forecast. Same pattern as the May 14 Brent paper-physical bifurcation, now in shipping. **Reversibility**: Paper unwind (-3.0% Frontline today) reverses on Pakistan-track failure or Israeli unilateral kinetic action. **Physical fundamentals (Q2 TCE $181,700/day) are CONTRACTED and don't reverse** — they print as Q2 earnings regardless of where Brent goes by August. **AWRP ~1% holding from May 19 levels** (8x pre-war) — insurers have NOT bought the deal-anticipation tape yet; physical premium intact. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **BOAT $41.36 -2.2% / Frontline $36.66 -1.1% / +20.0% 3M** carries forward from Friday closes. Markets dossier: Evercore tanker downgrade on 2027-28 oversupply remains driver (~70% crisis-unwind). **B5 VLCC TD3C and AWRP — no fresh data through Monday morning IST.** Baltic Exchange Week 18 partial WS408.13 ($370-410K/day estimated, ~9x pre-war); Week 19 not yet released through Monday. Marine war risk premiums hold at 1.5-3% (some 10%) Gulf H&M — ~8-20x pre-war 0.1-0.15%; JWC high-risk-zone designation unchanged. **Watch Tuesday-Wednesday for Baltic Exchange Week 19 print** — sustained move above WS500 / >$400K/day = operational confirmation of the Brent-paper move. **The Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates) is the bid signal for the physical-shipping leg** — if Tuesday strike order, BOAT/FRO snap back violently with rate-spike thesis confirmed. **Paper-physical bifurcation continues within FRO**: equity selling on Evercore 2027-28 oversupply downgrade while spot VLCC TD3C at $370-410K = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within same company's price discovery. **Houthi Red Sea Day 7 of 7 negative-space window CLOSES UNTRIPPED** today — no second incident since May 11 Hodeidah. **PRIOR (May 17): MARKETS CLOSED SUNDAY — OPERATIVE PRICES ARE FRIDAY MAY 15 CLOSES: BOAT $41.36 (-2.2% 1D, +1.3% 1M, +8.7% 3M); Frontline $36.66 (-1.1% 1D, +4.3% 1M, +20.0% 3M).** Per markets dossier C11: 'Frontline (Tankers) -1.1% 1D despite VLCC TD3C ~9x pre-war' — Evercore ISI downgrade May 13 citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply. **Paper-physical bifurcation WITHIN THE SAME COMPANY'S PRICE DISCOVERY** — Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING. Either equity is the leading indicator of de-escalation (rate spike short-lived) OR the rate spike continues and Frontline equity is the underpriced trade of the cycle. **A6 VLCC TD3C BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 18 PARTIAL WS408.13 (~9x pre-war) carries forward as the most recent rate print; Week 19 data NOT yet released through May 17 morning.** **No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 17 morning IST**; no second commercial-vessel seizure since May 14 Hui Chuan + Haj Ali. **Monday May 18 binary**: VLCC TD3C Week 19 release — sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical bifurcation terminal-escalation trigger fires. **If NYT preparation report operationalizes Monday, BOAT and FRO snap back violently with the rate-spike thesis confirmed.** **PRIOR (May 16):** VLCC TD3C BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 18 PARTIAL PRINT IS THE MOST RECENT (WS408.13, ~9x pre-war); WEEK 19 DATA NOT YET AVAILABLE THROUGH MAY 16 MORNING**. The paper-physical bifurcation that peaked May 14 (VLCC TD3C $467K/day Week 17 vs paper-Brent $105.68 then) has RESOLVED VIOLENTLY into physical confirmation on May 15: Brent closed $109.26 +3.3% / WTI $105.42 +4.2% (WTI booked +11% weekly gain — cleanest week of physical-tightness reflection in cycle). **A7 No fresh UKMTO advisory through May 16 morning** on Hui Chuan or Haj Ali; Iran has not commented; no attribution on Haj Ali; India MEA 'unacceptable' carries forward. No second commercial-vessel seizure near Fujairah or in Qatari EEZ. **A8 SELECTIVE HORMUZ REOPENING — Iran allowed 30+ Chinese ships to transit since May 13 night under 'Iranian management protocols'** (REPORTED Fars/IRGC-Navy primary; Iran state TV; Xinhua; Al Jazeera; CBS; Defense Post; Newsweek). Defense Post and Newsweek noted Iran has occasionally allowed Chinese ships even during the broader blockade because China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil. **Frontline FRO $36.66 (-1.1% 1D, +4.3% 1M, +20.0% 3M)**: equity stayed sold on de-escalation conviction even as physical-shipping rates stayed elevated. **BOAT ETF $41.36 (-2.2% 1D, +1.3% 1M, +8.7% 3M)** — sector-wide unwind despite physical VLCC strength. **Container Freight FBX Asia-NEur ~$2,850/FEU (+10% week); Shanghai-Jebel Ali $8,000+ (4x pre-war) per Freightos May 12** — Hormuz a 'regional' not systemic container shock per Freightos. **Marine War Risk (Gulf): 1-5% H&M (some quotes 5-10%); ~8-10x pre-war 0.1-0.15% — JMIC May 4-5 Hormuz Critical; 6 transits May 3 / 5 transits May 4 vs 138 historical.** LMA: 88% of underwriters retain Gulf appetite; safety, not availability, halts transit. **The Aramco ~30/day Hormuz transit benchmark is now partially explained by Iran-permitted Chinese transit — non-Iranian non-Chinese transit rate is likely LOWER than 30/day headline.** PRIOR (May 15): **A10 VLCC TD3C TANKER RATE NOW CONFIRMED VIA BALTIC EXCHANGE WEEK 17 AT WS459.28 / TCE $467,408/day** (CONFIRMED — Baltic Exchange Week 17 Tanker Report independent operational primary). Week 4 (late January) had TD3C at WS126.5 / TCE $112,394/day = today's rate is ~4.2x late-January reading and ~12-15x pre-war ~$30-40K range. **Week 18 partial data shows WS408.13 (Lloyd's: 'steepest one-day plunge in over half-decade')** — note the decline but rate still ~9x pre-war. **The 'VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions' trigger added yesterday at 'watching' is now BASIS CONFIRMED via Baltic Exchange ($467K is far above $200K threshold); 5-session sustain remains pending daily-print verification — recommend `watching` upgrade to `confirmed_basis_pending_sustain`**. **C3 FRONTLINE $37.07 (-0.3% 1D, +4.5% 1M, +21.3% 3M) — cross-section confirmation: equity is selling on de-escalation conviction (Evercore ISI 2027-28 oversupply downgrade was immediate cause of yesterday's -3.5%) while spot rate is at multi-year highs. The two are pricing different worlds; one is wrong.** BOAT ETF $42.28 (-0.3% 1D, +3.0% 1M, +11.1% 3M). **Freightos FBX01 China-USWC ~$2,127/FEU +4% WoW; Transpac +$1,000/FEU vs pre-war.** Marine war risk (Gulf): ~1.0% of H&M value (some transits 0.8% post-NCB), ~8x pre-war 0.10-0.15% — premiums now quoted in % of hull value, not bps; Hormuz 'CRITICAL' — 5-6 transits/day vs 138 historical. **CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61.** PRIOR (May 14): Frontline -3.5% / Evercore ISI downgrade / paper-physical bifurcation within same company. Evercore ISI downgrade May 13 citing 2027-28 orderbook oversupply against **SPOT VLCC TD3C ~$420-462K/day per Lloyd's List May 11 'in unchartered territory'** (vs graph's last-recorded ~$100K = MAJOR ESCALATION SIGNAL). Analyst selling forward oversupply; spot at multi-year highs. **Frontline equity SELLING vs Frontline tanker-day-rate SPIKING = paper-physical bifurcation playing out within SAME company's price discovery — investor should treat Frontline equity as leading indicator of de-escalation if it stays sold; treat VLCC TD3C as leading indicator of physical disruption if it stays elevated.** Per markets dossier H reversibility map: Frontline $32-34 (-9-14%) if MOU signs by May 21; **Frontline $50-55 (+34-48%) if Hormuz remains closed through June — VLCC at $420K supports the upside case**. **NEW TRIGGER (added on brent-crude node): VLCC TD3C sustained above $200K/day for 5+ sessions = paper-physical divergence terminal escalation**. BOAT ETF $42.41 (-0.3% 1D, +3.6% 1M, +11.5% 3M). **Container freight (FBX13): China-Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w); Asia-N.Europe $2,707/FEU (+11% w/w); Asia-Med $3,850/FEU (+15% w/w) — Mediterranean +15% w/w consistent with Red Sea + Hormuz rerouting persisting.** Tanker-container divergence persists. **AWRP ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war).** CENTCOM redirect tally unchanged at 61. PRIOR (May 13): Tanker market diverging from container market: FBX13 China/East Asia → Mediterranean $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) — capacity caught up; tanker market diverging. BOAT $42.56 (-0.5% 1D, +4.0% 1M, +15.9% 3M). **B17 VLCC AGIOS FANOURIOS I (Iraqi Basra crude, bound Vietnam Nghi Son) REVERSED COURSE BACK INTO GULF OF OMAN AT ~5 KNOTS WITHOUT CROSSING US BLOCKADE LINE — VISIBLE CONFIDENCE-COLLAPSE SIGNATURE EVEN FOR COMPLIANT NON-IRANIAN VLCCs as diplomatic posture deteriorates** (Bloomberg + gCaptain May 11). **A11 ARAMCO CEO NASSER MAY 11: 2-5 ships/day Hormuz (DETERIORATED from May 10 ~6/day), 100M bbl/week lost, 880M cumulative, market won't normalize until 2027 if disruption persists past mid-June** — industry-CEO authority on numbers. **B15 SAFESEA NEHA ATTRIBUTION: May 10 Doha bulk carrier was Marshall Islands-flagged Safesea Neha, US-managed Safesea Group NJ (UN peacekeeping/WFP/GSA logistics); 2nd Safesea vessel hit cycle (Safesea Vishnu March 12 Iraqi waters killed one Indian crew); NO claim; Pravda attribution to 2 Iranian UAVs UNCONFIRMED.** **A9 HOUTHI DAY 80 RED SEA QUIET POSSIBLY BROKEN MAY 11** — UKMTO confirmed multiple explosions near vessel 130km SW of Hodeidah; no claim. AWRP unchanged ~1% H&M / 7-day (8x pre-war 0.1-0.15%) — trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 6 of 10 FAILING HARD. CENTCOM 58→61 redirected + 4 disabled holds (3 redirects in 48h = below early-May pace, Project Freedom paused since May 5/6). VLCC TD3C ~$100K/day baseline; Baltic VLCC US Gulf-China $91,731/day (Lloyd's early May); Frontline secured $76,900/day starting 2026 contract. **Reversibility shifting: paper-market sell-off (Frontline -3.4% 1D on Evercore note) co-existing with physical-market tightening (VLCC U-turn, Hormuz 2-5 ships/day) = PAPER-PHYSICAL BIFURCATION DEEPENED.**

current.price: Frontline $34.71 (-2.1% 1D — tanker spot equities softening on deal-anticipation); VLCC Gulf-China ~$100K/day ($91.7K index), stabilized ~3 weeks; crude-on-VLCC volumes still -36% vs prewar; strait shut 71+ days (Day 90 of blockade); war-risk insurance ~1% of hull NOT unwinding.
current.delta_1d: Frontline -2.1% — the EQUITY expression of the crisis trade softening on deal hopes while physical (-36% volumes, ~$100K/day rates, Q2 TCE locked, insurance ~8x) stays impaired.
current.vlcc_economics: Frontline Q1 record VLCC spot TCE $103,500/day; Q2 contracted 82% of VLCC days @ $181,700/day (+75% sequential — physical book structurally bid above spot). Hormuz crisis cuts volumes -36% (14.4m bpd avg vs 22.5m prewar) but voyages longer (Cape rerouting) — Atlantic-to-Pacific share now 35% vs 22% baseline. Tonne-miles dislocation structural.
current.war_risk_differential: US/UK/Israeli vessels charged 3x non-aligned (Lloyd's List); war-risk insurance 3-8% of hull NOT unwinding (8x+ pre-war) — the physical floor under any paper relief.

active_triggers.condition: Houthis begin confirmed Red Sea attacks (verified by maritime trackers)
Lloyd's AWRP Persian Gulf rate falls below 0.8% for 5 consecutive sessions (Branch 3 deepening test)

recent_signals.headline: **PHYSICAL FLOOR HELD THROUGH THE RE-ESCALATION — STRAIT ~4 SHIPS/DAY, WAR-RISK INSURANCE ~60x NOT UNWINDING, VLCC AG WAR PREMIUM INTACT; PAPER WHIPSAWED ±7%.** Strait effectively closed at ~4 vessels/day (CNN '94 days'); blockade enforcement continued (US Hellfire into a commercial ship's engine room — continued enforcement, do not double-count tally). VLCC AG-China ~$92-100K/day stable, ~3x WAFR/USG; war-risk insurance $10-14M/transit, 2.5-5%, ~60x pre-crisis, NOT unwinding (DFC $40bn backstop); container freight elevated on Red Sea reroutes. BOAT $40.69 (-0.2% 1D), Frontline $34.62 (-0.7% 1D) — paper whipsawed on the Brent +7%-then-fade while the physical book stays locked. No confirmed new Houthi attack — only Bab el-Mandeb 'activation' rhetoric. Slow physical legs are the structural floor under oil's premium. No trigger moves.
sources: CNN (2 June) ~4 ships/day; Baltic Exchange / Breakwave (VLCC AG ~$92-100K/day, ~3x)
Lloyd's List / Howden / WEF (war-risk $10-14M/transit, 2.5-5%, ~60x, DFC $40bn backstop)

recent_signals.headline: **PHYSICAL VOLUMES IMPAIRED, PAPER SOFTENING ON DEAL HOPES — VLCC GULF-CHINA ~$100K/DAY ($91.7K INDEX), STABILIZED ~3 WEEKS; CRUDE-ON-VLCC VOLUMES STILL -36% VS PREWAR; STRAIT SHUT 71+ DAYS (DAY 90 OF BLOCKADE). recent_signals.headline: **FRONTLINE +2.9% / BOAT +1.2% — TON-MILES BID INVERSE TO SPOT OIL; HORMUZ REROUTE EXTENDS VOYAGE DISTANCE; STRUCTURAL-BID TRIO LEAD.** Hormuz remains effectively closed (~1-30 vessels/day vs 138 pre-war) → US Gulf/Atlantic crude must travel longer routes to Asia/Europe (Cape rerouting) → ton-miles dislocation → VLCC day rates ELEVATED even as Baltic prints normalize off the Week 17 WS459 peak (now WS133/$117K/day per Week 7 latest visible). Arctic Securities upgraded FRO/DHT/INSW/NAT in April on the 'reopening bonanza' thesis. **The trade**: lower spot Brent (less force-majeure tail) PLUS sustained ton-mile dislocation = tanker stocks unhedged-long. Baker Hughes call: full Hormuz reopen NOT until 2H26. **Frontline's pattern of leading BOAT on Brent-down days is now structurally embedded and is the clearest 'war premium migrated to ton-miles' signal.**
